title: podman generate kube: Error: containers with dependencies: not yet implemented
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

I would like to generate a YAML file for a pod with three containers. Following the documentation, the following should just work:

> podman generate kube django-ctr -f pod.yaml

with podman inspect I have:
```

{
     "Id": "55488ce603f7f5a02aee718f1cee86189d1a6b4802f8a6c0cd4543bef18bf4f1",
     "Name": "django-pod",
     "Created": "2021-04-22T10:16:21.151967446+03:00",
     "CreateCommand": [
          "podman",
          "pod",
          "create",
          "-p",
          "8000",
          "-p",
          "5432",
          "-n",
          "django-pod"
     ],
     "State": "Exited",
     "Hostname": "django-pod",
     "CreateCgroup": true,
     "CgroupParent": "/libpod_parent",
     "CgroupPath": "/libpod_parent/55488ce603f7f5a02aee718f1cee86189d1a6b4802f8a6c0cd4543bef18bf4f1",
     "CreateInfra": true,
     "InfraContainerID": "7e4030806efae23242201110ffaf4179aeb62167b1f985e0bf06c0de2e357cf1",
     "InfraConfig": {
          "PortBindings": {
               "5432/tcp": [
     {
          "HostIp": "",
          "HostPort": "39877"
     }
],
               "8000/tcp": [
     {
          "HostIp": "",
          "HostPort": "41353"
     }
]
          },
          "HostNetwork": false,
          "StaticIP": "",
          "StaticMAC": "",
          "NoManageResolvConf": false,
          "DNSServer": null,
          "DNSSearch": null,
          "DNSOption": null,
          "NoManageHosts": false,
          "HostAdd": null,
          "Networks": null,
          "NetworkOptions": null
     },
     "SharedNamespaces": [
          "net",
          "uts",
          "ipc"
     ],
     "NumContainers": 3,
     "Containers": [
          {
               "Id": "12bc0b3e05b9afd2d1ad2c6a541ecd001e372726b6d85d04cd6c8122a5a9b618",
               "Name": "django-ctr",
               "State": "stopped"
          },
          {
               "Id": "7e4030806efae23242201110ffaf4179aeb62167b1f985e0bf06c0de2e357cf1",
               "Name": "55488ce603f7-infra",
               "State": "stopped"
          },
          {
               "Id": "f5b2e921ce7d389e64b66a82fc312eaf5189ed5e5f6c33a27ae3674677583fd2",
               "Name": "postgresql-ctr",
               "State": "exited"
          }
     ]
}
```

**Describe the results you received:**

> podman generate kube django-ctr -f pod.yaml

Error: containers with dependencies: not yet implemented

**Describe the results you expected:**

A generated YAML file 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I have tried the above command both after starting and stopping the pod.

**Output of `podman version`:**

3.1.0

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```

```
**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.1.0-3.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

comments.body: You should give the pod name as argument to generate kube: `podman generate kube django-pod -f pod.yaml`
